column:addresses.address_code.description: An alphanumeric code that serves as the business-facing identifier for each address record. It uniquely identifies an address and can be used to reference or look up a specific location across systems. The format appears to be a fixed-length encoded string.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.address_code.name: address_code
terms: an alphanumeric code that serves as the business facing identifier for each address record. it uniquely identifies an address and can be used to reference or look up a specific location across systems. the format appears to be a fixed length encoded string.

column:addresses.address_record.description: A system-generated numeric identifier that uniquely identifies each row in the addresses table. This is an internal record key used to join address data to clients, purchases, and refunds. It does not carry direct business meaning on its own.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.address_record.name: address_record
terms: a system generated numeric identifier that uniquely identifies each row in the addresses table. this is an internal record key used to join address data to clients, purchases, and refunds. it does not carry direct business meaning on its own.

column:addresses.city.description: The city name associated with the client's address. Used for geographic segmentation and regional analysis of customers, purchases, and returns. The dataset includes 695 distinct cities across the United States. A small percentage of records have no city on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.city.name: city
terms: the city name associated with the client's address. used for geographic segmentation and regional analysis of customers, purchases, and returns. the dataset includes 695 distinct cities across the united states. a small percentage of records have no city on file.

column:addresses.country.description: The country associated with the client's address. All populated records in this dataset contain 'United States', indicating the business serves a domestic US customer base. A small percentage of records have no country value on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.country.name: country
terms: the country associated with the client's address. all populated records in this dataset contain 'united states', indicating the business serves a domestic us customer base. a small percentage of records have no country value on file.

column:addresses.county.description: Stores the county name for the client's address, such as 'Washington County' or 'Jefferson County'. Useful for sub-state geographic analysis. The dataset includes over 1,800 distinct counties. A small percentage of records have no county on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.county.name: county
terms: stores the county name for the client's address, such as 'washington county' or 'jefferson county'. useful for sub state geographic analysis. the dataset includes over 1,800 distinct counties. a small percentage of records have no county on file.

column:addresses.location_type.description: Classifies the residential dwelling type associated with the address. Values are apartment, condo, or single family. The three types are roughly equally distributed across the dataset. This attribute can be used to segment customers by housing type for demographic or marketing analysis. A small percentage of records have no location type on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.location_type.name: location_type

column:addresses.state.description: The two-letter US state abbreviation for the client's address, such as TX, GA, or VA. Covers all 50 US states plus Washington DC. Used for state-level geographic segmentation and regional sales analysis. A small percentage of records have no state on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.state.name: state
terms: the two letter us state abbreviation for the client's address, such as tx, ga, or va. covers all 50 us states plus washington dc. used for state level geographic segmentation and regional sales analysis. a small percentage of records have no state on file.

column:addresses.street_name.description: Contains the street name portion of the address, such as 'Main', 'Oak', or 'Park'. Common street names appear frequently across the dataset. A small percentage of records have no street name on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.street_name.name: street_name

column:addresses.street_number.description: Stores the street number component of a client's address, representing the house, building, or unit number on a street. Values range from 1 to 999. A small percentage of records have no street number on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.street_number.name: street_number

column:addresses.street_type.description: Describes the street type or suffix associated with the address, for example Avenue, Boulevard, Drive, Lane, Road, Street, or Way. Both abbreviated and full forms are present (e.g., 'Ave' and 'Avenue'). A small percentage of records have no street type on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.street_type.name: street_type
terms: describes the street type or suffix associated with the address, for example avenue, boulevard, drive, lane, road, street, or way. both abbreviated and full forms are present (e.g., 'ave' and 'avenue'). a small percentage of records have no street type on file.

column:addresses.suite_number.description: Stores the suite or unit identifier for addresses that include a secondary location within a building, such as 'Suite A' or 'Suite 100'. Not all addresses have a suite number; a small percentage of records have this field blank.

column:addresses.timezone_offset.description: The UTC offset in hours for the timezone associated with the address. Values range from -10.00 (Hawaii) to -5.00 (Eastern US), reflecting US time zones. The most common offsets are -6.00 (Central) and -5.00 (Eastern). Useful for analyzing purchase timing relative to local time. A small percentage of records have no timezone offset on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.timezone_offset.name: timezone_offset
terms: the utc offset in hours for the timezone associated with the address. values range from 10.00 (hawaii) to 5.00 (eastern us), reflecting us time zones. the most common offsets are 6.00 (central) and 5.00 (eastern). useful for analyzing purchase timing relative to local time. a small percentage of records have no timezone offset on file.

column:addresses.zip.description: The five-digit US postal ZIP code for the client's address. Supports fine-grained geographic analysis at the postal area level. The dataset includes nearly 3,700 distinct ZIP codes. A small percentage of records have no ZIP code on file.

metadata.table_grain: One row per unique address

column:addresses.zip.name: zip
terms: the five digit us postal zip code for the client's address. supports fine grained geographic analysis at the postal area level. the dataset includes nearly 3,700 distinct zip codes. a small percentage of records have no zip code on file.

Used to analyze whether purchase or activity timing correlates with meal periods.

column:household_profiles.buy_potential.description: The buy_potential field categorizes a household's estimated purchasing capacity into one of six spending bands: '0-500', '501-1000', '1001-5000', '5001-10000', '>10000', and 'Unknown'. Each band is equally represented across the household profile records.

column:household_profiles.vehicle_count.name: vehicle_count
terms: this field captures the number of vehicles associated with the household, with values ranging from 0 to 4. a value of 1 appears to indicate that vehicle ownership is unknown or not recorded for that household segment. vehicle count can serve as a proxy for household mobility or economic status and may be used in customer segmentation or lifestyle analysis. all six values ( 1 through 4) are equally distributed across the household profile records.

column:income_ranges.income_range_record.description: This is the primary key for the income_ranges table. It uniquely identifies each of the 20 income bracket records and is referenced by the income_range_ref field in the household_profiles table. It has no standalone business meaning beyond serving as the internal record identifier for the income band.

metadata.table_grain: One row per income band

column:income_ranges.income_range_record.name: income_range_record
terms: this is the primary key for the income ranges table. it uniquely identifies each of the 20 income bracket records and is referenced by the income range ref field in the household profiles table. it has no standalone business meaning beyond serving as the internal record identifier for the income band.

column:income_ranges.lower_bound.description: This field defines the lower boundary of an income bracket in dollars. Values range from $0 (the lowest band) to $190,001 (the highest band). Together with upper_bound, it defines a contiguous income range used to classify households into economic tiers. Analysts can use this field to filter or label income segments when studying how household income relates to purchasing behavior, campaign response, or customer lifetime value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per income band

column:income_ranges.lower_bound.name: lower_bound
terms: this field defines the lower boundary of an income bracket in dollars. values range from $0 (the lowest band) to $190,001 (the highest band). together with upper bound, it defines a contiguous income range used to classify households into economic tiers. analysts can use this field to filter or label income segments when studying how household income relates to purchasing behavior, campaign response, or customer lifetime value.

column:income_ranges.upper_bound.description: This field defines the upper boundary of an income bracket in dollars. Values range from $10,000 (the lowest band ceiling) to $200,000 (the highest band ceiling). Together with lower_bound, it forms a complete income range definition. The 20 bands are evenly spaced at $10,000 intervals. This field is used alongside lower_bound to label income segments in reports and dashboards that analyze customer spending by economic tier.

metadata.table_grain: One row per income band

column:income_ranges.upper_bound.name: upper_bound
terms: this field defines the upper boundary of an income bracket in dollars. values range from $10,000 (the lowest band ceiling) to $200,000 (the highest band ceiling). together with lower bound, it forms a complete income range definition. the 20 bands are evenly spaced at $10,000 intervals. this field is used alongside lower bound to label income segments in reports and dashboards that analyze customer spending by economic tier.

column:mail_order_purchases.billing_addr.description: This field references the addresses table and identifies the mailing or residential address used for billing on the mail-order order. It enables geographic analysis of mail-order revenue by city, state, ZIP code, or country based on the billing location. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.billing_addr.name: billing_address_ref
terms: this field references the addresses table and identifies the mailing or residential address used for billing on the mail order order. it enables geographic analysis of mail order revenue by city, state, zip code, or country based on the billing location. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.billing_clie.description: This field references the client_profiles table and captures the demographic segment (gender, marital status, education, credit rating, etc.) associated with the billing client at the time of the order. It enables segmentation of mail-order revenue and order volume by customer demographics. Because client profiles can change over time, this field preserves the profile that was active at the point of purchase.

column:mail_order_purchases.billing_clie.description: This field references the clients table and identifies the shopper who was billed for the mail-order order. In cases where the billing and shipping clients differ (e.g., a gift order), this field captures the paying customer while shipping_client_ref captures the recipient. It is the primary client link for revenue and customer-level purchase analysis in the mail-order channel.

terms: this field references the clients table and identifies the shopper who was billed for the mail order order. in cases where the billing and shipping clients differ (e.g., a gift order), this field captures the paying customer while shipping client ref captures the recipient. it is the primary client link for revenue and customer level purchase analysis in the mail order channel.

column:mail_order_purchases.billing_hous.description: This field references the household_profiles table and associates the billing client's household segment (income range, buying potential, dependents, vehicles) with the mail-order transaction. It supports analysis of mail-order purchasing behavior by household economic profile and lifestyle segment. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.campaign_ref.description: This field references the marketing_campaigns table and links the mail-order transaction to the campaign or promotional offer that influenced the sale. All 300 campaigns are represented in the mail-order purchase data. It is used to measure campaign ROI, compare order volumes across promotions, and analyze the impact of discounts or offers on mail-order revenue. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value, indicating orders not attributed to a specific campaign.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.campaign_ref.name: campaign_ref
terms: this field references the marketing campaigns table and links the mail order transaction to the campaign or promotional offer that influenced the sale. all 300 campaigns are represented in the mail order purchase data. it is used to measure campaign roi, compare order volumes across promotions, and analyze the impact of discounts or offers on mail order revenue. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value, indicating orders not attributed to a specific campaign.

column:mail_order_purchases.coupon_amoun.description: This field records the dollar value of any coupon or voucher applied to the order line. The vast majority of rows (~80%) have a coupon amount of $0.00, indicating that coupons are used on a minority of mail-order transactions. When applied, coupon values range up to over $26,000 for large-quantity orders. It is used to measure coupon redemption rates and the financial impact of coupon promotions. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.coupon_amoun.name: coupon_amount
terms: this field records the dollar value of any coupon or voucher applied to the order line. the vast majority of rows (~80%) have a coupon amount of $0.00, indicating that coupons are used on a minority of mail order transactions. when applied, coupon values range up to over $26,000 for large quantity orders. it is used to measure coupon redemption rates and the financial impact of coupon promotions. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.delivery_met.description: This field references the delivery_methods table and identifies the carrier, service type, and contract used to ship the mail-order order. All 20 delivery methods are used with roughly equal frequency across mail-order transactions. It supports analysis of delivery cost, carrier preference, and fulfillment efficiency. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.delivery_met.name: delivery_method_ref
terms: this field references the delivery methods table and identifies the carrier, service type, and contract used to ship the mail order order. all 20 delivery methods are used with roughly equal frequency across mail order transactions. it supports analysis of delivery cost, carrier preference, and fulfillment efficiency. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_del.description: This field records the total delivery or shipping cost associated with the order line, ranging from $0.00 (free shipping) to over $14,000 for large-quantity orders. A small number of rows have $0.00 delivery cost, suggesting some orders qualify for free shipping. It is used to analyze shipping cost by delivery method, fulfillment center, or order size, and is included in net-paid-with-delivery calculations. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_del.name: extended_delivery_cost
terms: this field records the total delivery or shipping cost associated with the order line, ranging from $0.00 (free shipping) to over $14,000 for large quantity orders. a small number of rows have $0.00 delivery cost, suggesting some orders qualify for free shipping. it is used to analyze shipping cost by delivery method, fulfillment center, or order size, and is included in net paid with delivery calculations. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_dis.description: This field captures the total dollar value of discounts applied to the order line, calculated as the per-unit discount multiplied by quantity. Values range from $0.00 (no discount) to over $28,000 for high-quantity orders. It is used to measure promotional generosity, analyze discount impact on revenue, and evaluate campaign effectiveness. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_dis.name: extended_discount_amount
terms: this field captures the total dollar value of discounts applied to the order line, calculated as the per unit discount multiplied by quantity. values range from $0.00 (no discount) to over $28,000 for high quantity orders. it is used to measure promotional generosity, analyze discount impact on revenue, and evaluate campaign effectiveness. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_lis.description: This field records the total undiscounted value of the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per-unit list price. It ranges from $1.27 to nearly $30,000. Comparing extended_list_price to extended_sales_price reveals the total discount value applied to the line. It is useful for measuring promotional depth and potential revenue foregone. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_lis.name: extended_list_price
terms: this field records the total undiscounted value of the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per unit list price. it ranges from $1.27 to nearly $30,000. comparing extended list price to extended sales price reveals the total discount value applied to the line. it is useful for measuring promotional depth and potential revenue foregone. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_sal.description: This field records the total sales revenue for the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per-unit sales price after discounts. It ranges from $0.00 to over $28,000. It is the primary revenue measure at the line-item level for mail-order sales analysis. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_sal.name: extended_sales_price
terms: this field records the total sales revenue for the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per unit sales price after discounts. it ranges from $0.00 to over $28,000. it is the primary revenue measure at the line item level for mail order sales analysis. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_tax.description: This field records the total tax charged on the order line. A large proportion of rows have a tax value of $0.00, suggesting that many mail-order transactions are tax-exempt or shipped to non-taxable jurisdictions. Non-zero values range up to over $2,400 for high-value orders. It is used in net-paid-with-tax calculations. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_tax.name: extended_tax
terms: this field records the total tax charged on the order line. a large proportion of rows have a tax value of $0.00, suggesting that many mail order transactions are tax exempt or shipped to non taxable jurisdictions. non zero values range up to over $2,400 for high value orders. it is used in net paid with tax calculations. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_who.description: This field records the total acquisition cost for the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per-unit wholesale cost. It ranges from just over $1.00 to $9,999.00. It is used alongside extended_sales_price to calculate gross margin and profitability at the line-item level. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.extended_who.name: extended_wholesale_cost
terms: this field records the total acquisition cost for the order line, calculated as quantity multiplied by the per unit wholesale cost. it ranges from just over $1.00 to $9,999.00. it is used alongside extended sales price to calculate gross margin and profitability at the line item level. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.fulfillment_.description: This field references the fulfillment_centers table and identifies which of the five fulfillment centers dispatched the mail-order shipment. All five centers handle roughly equal volumes of mail-order orders. It supports analysis of fulfillment center workload, shipping costs by location, and inventory utilization. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.fulfillment_.name: fulfillment_center_ref
terms: this field references the fulfillment centers table and identifies which of the five fulfillment centers dispatched the mail order shipment. all five centers handle roughly equal volumes of mail order orders. it supports analysis of fulfillment center workload, shipping costs by location, and inventory utilization. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.list_price.description: This field records the published or catalog list price per unit of the merchandise item, ranging from $1.01 to $300.00. It represents the full undiscounted price before any promotions or coupons are applied. Comparing list_price to sales_price reveals the discount depth applied to the order line. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.list_price.name: list_price
terms: this field records the published or catalog list price per unit of the merchandise item, ranging from $1.01 to $300.00. it represents the full undiscounted price before any promotions or coupons are applied. comparing list price to sales price reveals the discount depth applied to the order line. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.mailer_page_.description: This field references the mailer_pages table and identifies the specific catalog or mailer page associated with the order. It is a key dimension for analyzing the effectiveness of print marketing materials in driving mail-order sales. By linking purchases back to mailer pages, analysts can evaluate which catalog sections, departments, or mailer editions generate the most revenue or order volume. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.mailer_page_.name: mailer_page_ref
terms: this field references the mailer pages table and identifies the specific catalog or mailer page associated with the order. it is a key dimension for analyzing the effectiveness of print marketing materials in driving mail order sales. by linking purchases back to mailer pages, analysts can evaluate which catalog sections, departments, or mailer editions generate the most revenue or order volume. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.merchandise_.description: This field references the merchandise table and identifies the specific product item included in the mail-order order line. It is a required field with no null values, confirming that every mail-order purchase row is associated with a product. It enables analysis of mail-order sales by product, brand, category, class, or manufacturer, and supports product-level revenue and profitability reporting.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.merchandise_.name: merchandise_ref
terms: this field references the merchandise table and identifies the specific product item included in the mail order order line. it is a required field with no null values, confirming that every mail order purchase row is associated with a product. it enables analysis of mail order sales by product, brand, category, class, or manufacturer, and supports product level revenue and profitability reporting.

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid.description: This field records the total amount paid by the customer for the order line after discounts and coupons, but before tax and delivery costs are added. It ranges from $0.00 to over $28,000. It is the core revenue measure for mail-order sales analysis and is the basis for the tax and delivery variants (net_paid_with_tax, net_paid_with_delivery). A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid.name: net_paid
terms: this field records the total amount paid by the customer for the order line after discounts and coupons, but before tax and delivery costs are added. it ranges from $0.00 to over $28,000. it is the core revenue measure for mail order sales analysis and is the basis for the tax and delivery variants (net paid with tax, net paid with delivery). a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid_wit.description: This field adds the extended delivery cost to net_paid, giving the total customer payment inclusive of shipping but before tax. It ranges from $0.00 to over $40,000. It is used to analyze the total cost to the customer when delivery fees are included, and to evaluate the impact of delivery pricing on overall order value. This field has no null values.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid_wit.name: net_paid_with_delivery
terms: column:mail order purchases.net paid with delivery
mail order purchases.net paid with delivery

this field adds the extended delivery cost to net paid, giving the total customer payment inclusive of shipping but before tax. it ranges from $0.00 to over $40,000. it is used to analyze the total cost to the customer when delivery fees are included, and to evaluate the impact of delivery pricing on overall order value. this field has no null values.

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid_wit.description: This field represents the total amount paid by the customer for the order line, inclusive of all charges: the sales price after discounts and coupons, plus delivery costs, plus tax. It ranges from $0.00 to over $41,000 and is the most complete measure of customer spend at the line-item level for mail-order transactions. It is used in total revenue and customer spend analyses. This field has no null values.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid_wit.description: This field adds the extended tax amount to net_paid, giving the total customer payment inclusive of tax but before delivery costs. It ranges from $0.00 to over $30,000. It is used in revenue reporting where tax-inclusive totals are required. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.net_paid_wit.name: net_paid_with_tax
terms: column:mail order purchases.net paid with tax
mail order purchases.net paid with tax

this field adds the extended tax amount to net paid, giving the total customer payment inclusive of tax but before delivery costs. it ranges from $0.00 to over $30,000. it is used in revenue reporting where tax inclusive totals are required. a small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

column:mail_order_purchases.net_profit.description: This field records the net profit (or loss) generated by the order line, calculated as the difference between what the customer paid and the wholesale cost of the merchandise. Values range from approximately -$9,897 (a loss) to nearly $19,000 (a gain), with negative values indicating that the item was sold below cost, often due to heavy discounting or coupon usage. It is the primary profitability metric for mail-order line-item analysis and is used to evaluate margin performance by product, campaign, customer segment, or fulfillment center. This field has no null values.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.net_profit.name: net_profit
terms: this field records the net profit (or loss) generated by the order line, calculated as the difference between what the customer paid and the wholesale cost of the merchandise. values range from approximately $9,897 (a loss) to nearly $19,000 (a gain), with negative values indicating that the item was sold below cost, often due to heavy discounting or coupon usage. it is the primary profitability metric for mail order line item analysis and is used to evaluate margin performance by product, campaign, customer segment, or fulfillment center. this field has no null values.

column:mail_order_purchases.order_number.description: This field contains the order number that groups individual product line items into a single customer order. With 160,000 distinct order numbers across 1.4 million rows, each order typically contains multiple line items. It is used to aggregate order-level totals, count distinct orders, and analyze basket size or multi-item purchasing behavior in the mail-order channel. This field has no null values.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.quantity.description: This field records how many units of the merchandise item were included in the order line, with values ranging from 1 to 100. It is used in conjunction with per-unit price fields to calculate extended amounts. Quantity is a key driver of extended sales price, extended wholesale cost, and extended delivery cost. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per mail-order transaction

column:mail_order_purchases.sale_calenda.description: This field references the calendar_days table and identifies the specific date on which the mail-order sale occurred. It enables time-based analysis such as daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly sales trends for the mail-order channel. A small proportion of rows (~0.5%) have a null value for this field, which may indicate orders where the sale date was not recorded.

column:mail_order_purchases.sale_clock_t.description: This field references the clock_times table and captures the specific time of day (to the second) at which the mail-order order was placed. column:mail_order_purchases.sales_price.description: This field records the per-unit price actually charged to the customer after any discounts or promotions have been applied, ranging from $0.00 to $297.83. column:mail_order_purchases.shipping_add.description: This field references the addresses table and identifies the physical address to which the mail-order order was delivered. column:mail_order_purchases.shipping_cal.description: This field references the calendar_days table and records the date on which the mail-order order was dispatched for delivery. column:online_properties.online_property.description: A alphanumeric code that serves as the business identifier for an online property. Unlike online_property_record, which is unique per row, online_property_code groups all historical versions of the same website together. Use this field to track a site across time or to join to other tables that reference the online property by its business code.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.online_property.name: online_property_code
terms: a alphanumeric code that serves as the business identifier for an online property. unlike online property record, which is unique per row, online property code groups all historical versions of the same website together. use this field to track a site across time or to join to other tables that reference the online property by its business code.

column:online_properties.online_property.description: A unique numeric identifier assigned to each row in the online_properties table. Because the table uses slowly changing dimension logic, the same website may have multiple rows with different effective date ranges, each with its own online_property_record value. This is an internal system key; business users should use online_property_code or the site name to identify a specific website.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.online_property.name: online_property_record
terms: a unique numeric identifier assigned to each row in the online properties table. because the table uses slowly changing dimension logic, the same website may have multiple rows with different effective date ranges, each with its own online property record value. this is an internal system key; business users should use online property code or the site name to identify a specific website.

column:online_properties.opening_calenda.description: Links to the calendar_days table to identify the date on which the website or digital property was launched and began accepting online orders. This field supports analysis of site tenure, cohort comparisons by launch date, and filtering to properties that were active during a specific period.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.opening_calenda.name: opening_calendar_day_ref
terms: links to the calendar days table to identify the date on which the website or digital property was launched and began accepting online orders. this field supports analysis of site tenure, cohort comparisons by launch date, and filtering to properties that were active during a specific period.

column:online_properties.record_effectiv.description: The calendar date on which this version of the online property's attributes was superseded by a newer record. A null value means the row represents the current, active version of the property. Approximately half of all rows have a null end date, indicating they are the live records. Use this field alongside record_effective_start to perform point-in-time or current-state analysis of website properties.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.record_effectiv.name: record_effective_end
terms: the calendar date on which this version of the online property's attributes was superseded by a newer record. a null value means the row represents the current, active version of the property. approximately half of all rows have a null end date, indicating they are the live records. use this field alongside record effective start to perform point in time or current state analysis of website properties.

column:online_properties.record_effectiv.description: The calendar date on which this particular version of the online property's attributes took effect. Together with record_effective_end, this field defines the validity window for the row. When analyzing current website attributes, filter to rows where record_effective_start is on or before today and record_effective_end is null or after today.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.record_effectiv.name: record_effective_start
terms: the calendar date on which this particular version of the online property's attributes took effect. together with record effective end, this field defines the validity window for the row. when analyzing current website attributes, filter to rows where record effective start is on or before today and record effective end is null or after today.

column:online_properties.state.description: The US state abbreviation for the online property's registered address. All 30 records show 'TN' (Tennessee), consistent with the city and county data indicating all online properties are registered in the same state in this dataset.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.state.name: state

column:online_properties.street_name.description: The name of the street in the online property's registered address. All 30 properties have distinct street names, suggesting each property has a unique physical address on record. This field is part of the full address used for location-based analysis or regulatory purposes.

column:online_properties.suite_number.description: The suite or office unit designation within the building at the online property's registered address. terms: the suite or office unit designation within the building at the online property's registered address. all 30 records have a suite number, indicating these properties are located in multi tenant office buildings. this field is used for complete address formatting and mail delivery purposes.

column:online_properties.tax_percentage.description: The sales tax rate, expressed as a decimal percentage, applicable to transactions processed through the online property. Values range from 0.00 to 0.12 (0% to 12%), with 0.07 (7%) being the most common rate across 10 of the 30 properties. This field is used to calculate tax amounts on online purchases and supports revenue and tax reporting by website or market region.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.tax_percentage.name: tax_percentage
terms: the sales tax rate, expressed as a decimal percentage, applicable to transactions processed through the online property. values range from 0.00 to 0.12 (0% to 12%), with 0.07 (7%) being the most common rate across 10 of the 30 properties. this field is used to calculate tax amounts on online purchases and supports revenue and tax reporting by website or market region.

column:online_properties.timezone_offset.description: The numeric offset from UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) for the online property's registered location. All 30 records carry a value of -5.00, corresponding to Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5), consistent with the Tennessee address data. This field supports time-of-day analysis when correlating online purchase timestamps across time zones.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website property version (slowly changing)

column:online_properties.timezone_offset.name: timezone_offset
terms: the numeric offset from utc (coordinated universal time) for the online property's registered location. all 30 records carry a value of 5.00, corresponding to eastern standard time (utc 5), consistent with the tennessee address data. this field supports time of day analysis when correlating online purchase timestamps across time zones.

column:online_properties.zip.description: The postal ZIP code associated with the online property's registered address. Only two ZIP codes appear in the dataset (31904 and 35709), consistent with the limited number of cities. This field can be used for geographic filtering or tax jurisdiction identification.

column:online_purchases.billing_address_.name: billing_address_ref

column:online_purchases.billing_client_p.description: Links the billing customer to their demographic segment record in the client_profiles table, capturing attributes such as gender, marital status, education, and credit rating as they were at the time of the sale. metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

Use this to analyze purchasing behavior, customer lifetime value, and repeat-buyer patterns.

column:online_purchases.coupon_amount.description: The aggregate value of coupons redeemed against this line item. The majority of online purchases show a coupon amount of zero, indicating coupons are used selectively. When non-zero, this represents additional savings beyond any promotional discount already reflected in the sales price. Useful for analyzing coupon redemption rates and their impact on revenue.

column:online_purchases.delivery_method_.description: Links the purchase to a delivery method record in the delivery_methods table, identifying the carrier, service type, and contract used to ship the order. Use this to analyze shipping cost distribution, free shipping uptake, and delivery cost impact on profitability.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

column:online_purchases.extended_deliver.name: extended_delivery_cost

column:online_purchases.extended_discoun.description: The aggregate discount amount for the line item, calculated as the difference between the extended list price and the extended sales price across all units purchased. A value of zero indicates no discount was applied. Useful for measuring promotional depth and total savings given to customers on online orders.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

column:online_purchases.extended_discoun.name: extended_discount_amount
terms: the aggregate discount amount for the line item, calculated as the difference between the extended list price and the extended sales price across all units purchased. a value of zero indicates no discount was applied. useful for measuring promotional depth and total savings given to customers on online orders.

column:online_purchases.extended_list_pr.description: The aggregate standard retail value of all units in the line item, calculated as list_price multiplied by quantity. Comparing this to extended_sales_price shows the total discount given. Useful for measuring the face value of merchandise sold and the overall discount rate applied.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

column:online_purchases.extended_sales_p.description: The total amount charged to the customer for all units in this purchase line at the discounted sales price (sales_price multiplied by quantity). This is the primary revenue figure before tax and delivery costs are added. Use this for online channel revenue reporting and discount impact analysis.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

column:online_purchases.extended_sales_p.name: extended_sales_price

column:online_purchases.extended_tax.description: The aggregate tax amount applied to the line item. Used to calculate total customer cost including tax and to support tax reporting.

column:online_purchases.extended_wholesa.description: The aggregate cost of goods for the line item, calculated as wholesale_cost multiplied by quantity. column:online_purchases.net_paid.description: The total amount the customer paid for this purchase line after discounts and coupons are applied, but before tax and delivery costs are added. metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per online order

the fully loaded customer payment for this purchase line, incorporating the net sales amount, delivery costs, and applicable taxes. this represents the complete out of pocket cost to the shopper. use this as the all in revenue figure for customer facing total cost analysis and comprehensive online channel revenue reporting.

column:online_purchases.net_profit.description: The profit generated from this purchase line, calculated as the net revenue received minus the wholesale cost of goods. Can be negative when discounts, coupons, or delivery costs exceed the revenue collected, indicating a loss on the transaction. Ranges from approximately -$9,938 to +$18,865. Use this for online channel margin analysis, profitability by product or campaign, and identifying loss-making transactions.

column:retail_locations.zip.description: The five-digit US postal ZIP code for the store location. Two ZIP codes appear in the sample data, corresponding to the two cities (Midway and Fairview). Used for geographic segmentation and delivery zone analysis.

metadata.table_grain: One row per store version (slowly changing)

column:retail_locations.zip.name: zip
terms: the five digit us postal zip code for the store location. two zip codes appear in the sample data, corresponding to the two cities (midway and fairview). used for geographic segmentation and delivery zone analysis.

column:return_reasons.reason_description.description: A human-readable description of the return or refund reason. Examples include 'Did not fit', 'Wrong size', 'Package was damaged', 'Found a better price in a store', 'Stopped working', 'Gift exchange', 'Lost my job', and 'Unauthorized purchase'. This field is the primary attribute for analyzing return drivers, identifying product quality issues, understanding customer satisfaction, and benchmarking return rates by reason category.

metadata.table_grain: One row per return reason code

column:return_reasons.reason_description.name: reason_description
terms: a human readable description of the return or refund reason. examples include 'did not fit', 'wrong size', 'package was damaged', 'found a better price in a store', 'stopped working', 'gift exchange', 'lost my job', and 'unauthorized purchase'. this field is the primary attribute for analyzing return drivers, identifying product quality issues, understanding customer satisfaction, and benchmarking return rates by reason category.

column:return_reasons.return_reason_code.description: An alphanumeric code used to reference a specific return reason in cross-table joins and business reporting. Each code corresponds to one reason_description. This code appears as a foreign key in store_refunds, online_refunds, and mail_order_refunds tables.

metadata.table_grain: One row per return reason code

column:return_reasons.return_reason_code.name: return_reason_code
terms: an alphanumeric code used to reference a specific return reason in cross table joins and business reporting. each code corresponds to one reason description. this code appears as a foreign key in store refunds, online refunds, and mail order refunds tables.

column:return_reasons.return_reason_reco.description: A sequential numeric identifier serving as the internal primary key for the return_reasons table. It is used to join return reason records to refund fact tables across store, online, and mail-order channels. Business users should reference return_reason_code or reason_description to identify specific return reasons.

metadata.table_grain: One row per return reason code

column:return_reasons.return_reason_reco.name: return_reason_record
terms: a sequential numeric identifier serving as the internal primary key for the return reasons table. it is used to join return reason records to refund fact tables across store, online, and mail order channels. business users should reference return reason code or reason description to identify specific return reasons.

column:site_pages.access_calendar_day_re.description: Links to the calendar_days table to identify the last date the page was visited or accessed. Supports analysis of page recency, engagement timing, and identification of stale or inactive pages. All records have a non-null access date.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.access_calendar_day_re.name: access_calendar_day_ref
terms: links to the calendar days table to identify the last date the page was visited or accessed. supports analysis of page recency, engagement timing, and identification of stale or inactive pages. all records have a non null access date.

column:site_pages.auto_generated_flag.description: A yes/no flag (Y/N) distinguishing system-generated pages from manually authored ones. Approximately 35% of pages are auto-generated. This field supports content strategy analysis by separating programmatic pages (such as product listing pages) from hand-crafted editorial content.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.auto_generated_flag.name: auto_generated_flag
terms: a yes/no flag (y/n) distinguishing system generated pages from manually authored ones. approximately 35% of pages are auto generated. this field supports content strategy analysis by separating programmatic pages (such as product listing pages) from hand crafted editorial content.

column:site_pages.char_count.description: A measure of the text volume on the page, expressed as a character count. Values range from approximately 700 to 7,000 characters. This field supports content analysis, such as identifying whether longer pages correlate with higher engagement or conversion rates.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.char_count.name: char_count
terms: a measure of the text volume on the page, expressed as a character count. values range from approximately 700 to 7,000 characters. this field supports content analysis, such as identifying whether longer pages correlate with higher engagement or conversion rates.

column:site_pages.client_ref.description: References the clients table to associate a page with a particular shopper or account. Approximately 65% of pages have no client association (null), suggesting most pages are public or shared across all visitors. When populated, this field may indicate personalized or account-specific pages such as order confirmation or profile pages.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.client_ref.name: client_ref
terms: references the clients table to associate a page with a particular shopper or account. approximately 65% of pages have no client association (null), suggesting most pages are public or shared across all visitors. when populated, this field may indicate personalized or account specific pages such as order confirmation or profile pages.

column:site_pages.creation_calendar_day_.description: Links to the calendar_days table to identify the date the page was originally created or published. Supports analysis of page age, content freshness, and the timing of website expansion. A small number of records have a null creation date.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.creation_calendar_day_.name: creation_calendar_day_ref
terms: links to the calendar days table to identify the date the page was originally created or published. supports analysis of page age, content freshness, and the timing of website expansion. a small number of records have a null creation date.

column:site_pages.image_count.description: The total count of images on the page, ranging from 1 to 7. This field supports analysis of visual content richness and its potential impact on shopper engagement, conversion rates, and page load considerations.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.image_count.name: image_count
terms: the total count of images on the page, ranging from 1 to 7. this field supports analysis of visual content richness and its potential impact on shopper engagement, conversion rates, and page load considerations.

column:site_pages.link_count.description: The total count of clickable links on the page. Values range from 2 to 25 links. This field can be used to analyze page navigation complexity, internal linking strategy, and the relationship between link density and shopper behavior.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.link_count.name: link_count
terms: the total count of clickable links on the page. values range from 2 to 25 links. this field can be used to analyze page navigation complexity, internal linking strategy, and the relationship between link density and shopper behavior.

column:site_pages.max_ad_count.description: The upper limit of ad slots available on the page, ranging from 0 to 4. Pages with 0 carry no advertising. This field supports ad inventory analysis, revenue potential estimation from digital advertising, and decisions about which pages to monetize with promotional placements.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.max_ad_count.name: max_ad_count
terms: the upper limit of ad slots available on the page, ranging from 0 to 4. pages with 0 carry no advertising. this field supports ad inventory analysis, revenue potential estimation from digital advertising, and decisions about which pages to monetize with promotional placements.

column:site_pages.record_effective_end.description: The end date of the period during which this page record was active. A null value indicates the record is the current active version. Approximately half of all records are currently active (null end date), consistent with a slowly-changing-dimension pattern.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.record_effective_end.name: record_effective_end
terms: the end date of the period during which this page record was active. a null value indicates the record is the current active version. approximately half of all records are currently active (null end date), consistent with a slowly changing dimension pattern.

column:site_pages.record_effective_start.description: The start date of the period during which this page record's attributes were valid. Used together with record_effective_end to identify the current or historical version of a page's profile. Supports time-based analysis of page content changes.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.record_effective_start.name: record_effective_start
terms: the start date of the period during which this page record's attributes were valid. used together with record effective end to identify the current or historical version of a page's profile. supports time based analysis of page content changes.

column:site_pages.site_page_code.description: An alphanumeric code used to reference a website page in cross-table joins and reporting. Multiple rows may share the same code when a page's attributes have changed over time, reflecting the slowly-changing-dimension history of the table.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.site_page_code.name: site_page_code
terms: an alphanumeric code used to reference a website page in cross table joins and reporting. multiple rows may share the same code when a page's attributes have changed over time, reflecting the slowly changing dimension history of the table.

column:site_pages.site_page_record.description: A sequential numeric identifier serving as the internal primary key for the site_pages table. It is used to join page records to online purchase and refund transactions. Business users should use site_page_code or url to reference specific pages.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.site_page_record.name: site_page_record
terms: a sequential numeric identifier serving as the internal primary key for the site pages table. it is used to join page records to online purchase and refund transactions. business users should use site page code or url to reference specific pages.

column:site_pages.type.description: A classification label describing the purpose or function of the page. Observed values include 'general', 'welcome', 'protected', 'ad', 'dynamic', 'feedback', and 'order'. This field supports segmentation of web traffic and conversion analysis by page function, such as comparing engagement on ad pages versus order confirmation pages.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.type.name: type
terms: a classification label describing the purpose or function of the page. observed values include 'general', 'welcome', 'protected', 'ad', 'dynamic', 'feedback', and 'order'. this field supports segmentation of web traffic and conversion analysis by page function, such as comparing engagement on ad pages versus order confirmation pages.

column:site_pages.url.description: The full URL of the website page. In the sample data, all pages share the same placeholder URL, which likely reflects synthetic test data. In a production context, this field would contain distinct URLs used to identify and link to specific pages for traffic and conversion analysis.

metadata.table_grain: One row per website page version (slowly changing)

column:site_pages.url.name: url
terms: the full url of the website page. in the sample data, all pages share the same placeholder url, which likely reflects synthetic test data. in a production context, this field would contain distinct urls used to identify and link to specific pages for traffic and conversion analysis.

column:stock_levels.calendar_day_ref.description: Links to the calendar_days table to identify the specific date for which the stock quantity is recorded. Stock levels are captured at regular weekly intervals across a multi-year period. This field is essential for tracking inventory trends over time, identifying seasonal patterns, and comparing stock availability to purchase demand on specific dates.

metadata.table_grain: One row per product per fulfillment center per calendar day

column:stock_levels.calendar_day_ref.name: calendar_day_ref
terms: links to the calendar days table to identify the specific date for which the stock quantity is recorded. stock levels are captured at regular weekly intervals across a multi year period. this field is essential for tracking inventory trends over time, identifying seasonal patterns, and comparing stock availability to purchase demand on specific dates.

column:stock_levels.fulfillment_center_r.description: References the fulfillment_centers table to identify which warehouse holds the recorded stock quantity. All 5 fulfillment centers are represented equally in the data. Used to analyze inventory distribution across the warehouse network and to support fulfillment planning and stockout analysis by location.

metadata.table_grain: One row per product per fulfillment center per calendar day

column:stock_levels.fulfillment_center_r.name: fulfillment_center_ref
terms: references the fulfillment centers table to identify which warehouse holds the recorded stock quantity. all 5 fulfillment centers are represented equally in the data. used to analyze inventory distribution across the warehouse network and to support fulfillment planning and stockout analysis by location.

column:stock_levels.merchandise_ref.description: References the merchandise table to identify which product item the stock count applies to. All 18,000 merchandise items appear in the stock_levels table, enabling inventory analysis across the full product catalog. Used to join stock data with product attributes such as brand, category, and class.

metadata.table_grain: One row per product per fulfillment center per calendar day

column:stock_levels.merchandise_ref.name: merchandise_ref
terms: references the merchandise table to identify which product item the stock count applies to. all 18,000 merchandise items appear in the stock levels table, enabling inventory analysis across the full product catalog. used to join stock data with product attributes such as brand, category, and class.

column:stock_levels.quantity_on_hand.description: The count of product units physically available at a specific fulfillment center on a specific calendar day. Values range from 0 (out of stock) to 1,000 units. Approximately 5% of records have a null value, which may indicate missing data for certain product-location-date combinations. This is the primary metric for inventory availability analysis, stockout detection, replenishment planning, and supply-demand gap assessment.

metadata.table_grain: One row per product per fulfillment center per calendar day

column:stock_levels.quantity_on_hand.name: quantity_on_hand
terms: the count of product units physically available at a specific fulfillment center on a specific calendar day. values range from 0 (out of stock) to 1,000 units. approximately 5% of records have a null value, which may indicate missing data for certain product location date combinations. this is the primary metric for inventory availability analysis, stockout detection, replenishment planning, and supply demand gap assessment.

column:store_purchases.address_ref.description: Links the store purchase to a specific address record in the addresses dimension. Used to analyze in-store sales by customer geography, including city, state, ZIP code, and county.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.address_ref.name: address_ref
terms: links the store purchase to a specific address record in the addresses dimension. used to analyze in store sales by customer geography, including city, state, zip code, and county.

column:store_purchases.campaign_ref.description: Links the store purchase to a specific marketing campaign or promotional offer in the marketing_campaigns dimension. Used to measure campaign-driven sales, discount effectiveness, and promotional lift at the store level.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.campaign_ref.name: campaign_ref
terms: links the store purchase to a specific marketing campaign or promotional offer in the marketing campaigns dimension. used to measure campaign driven sales, discount effectiveness, and promotional lift at the store level.

column:store_purchases.client_profile_re.description: Links the store purchase to a specific demographic segment record in the client_profiles dimension, capturing attributes such as gender, marital status, education, and credit rating as they were at the time of the transaction. Used for demographic segmentation of in-store sales.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.client_profile_re.name: client_profile_ref
terms: links the store purchase to a specific demographic segment record in the client profiles dimension, capturing attributes such as gender, marital status, education, and credit rating as they were at the time of the transaction. used for demographic segmentation of in store sales.

column:store_purchases.client_ref.description: Links the store purchase to a specific shopper account in the clients dimension. Used to analyze purchase history, customer loyalty, and lifetime value for individual shoppers. May be null for anonymous or unidentified transactions.

column:store_purchases.client_ref.name: client_ref
terms: links the store purchase to a specific shopper account in the clients dimension. used to analyze purchase history, customer loyalty, and lifetime value for individual shoppers. may be null for anonymous or unidentified transactions.

column:store_purchases.coupon_amount.description: The total value of coupons redeemed on this purchase line. Most transactions show zero coupon usage. Used to measure coupon redemption rates, promotional savings, and the impact of coupon campaigns on revenue.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.coupon_amount.name: coupon_amount
terms: the total value of coupons redeemed on this purchase line. most transactions show zero coupon usage. used to measure coupon redemption rates, promotional savings, and the impact of coupon campaigns on revenue.

column:store_purchases.extended_discount.description: The total monetary discount given on this purchase line, calculated across the full quantity purchased. Represents the difference between the extended list price and the extended sales price attributable to discounting. Frequently zero when no discount applies.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.extended_discount.name: extended_discount_amount
terms: the total monetary discount given on this purchase line, calculated across the full quantity purchased. represents the difference between the extended list price and the extended sales price attributable to discounting. frequently zero when no discount applies.

column:store_purchases.extended_list_pri.description: The total standard retail value of this purchase line before any discounts, equal to the list price per unit multiplied by the quantity. Used to measure the full value of items sold and the total discount given.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.extended_list_pri.name: extended_list_price
terms: the total standard retail value of this purchase line before any discounts, equal to the list price per unit multiplied by the quantity. used to measure the full value of items sold and the total discount given.

column:store_purchases.extended_sales_pr.description: The total amount charged to the customer for this purchase line, equal to the sales price multiplied by the quantity. This is the primary revenue measure at the line-item level before tax and after discounts.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.extended_sales_pr.name: extended_sales_price
terms: the total amount charged to the customer for this purchase line, equal to the sales price multiplied by the quantity. this is the primary revenue measure at the line item level before tax and after discounts.

column:store_purchases.extended_tax.description: The total tax amount applied to this purchase line. Many transactions carry zero tax, reflecting tax-exempt items or locations. Used to analyze tax liability and to reconcile net paid amounts with and without tax.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.extended_tax.name: extended_tax
terms: the total tax amount applied to this purchase line. many transactions carry zero tax, reflecting tax exempt items or locations. used to analyze tax liability and to reconcile net paid amounts with and without tax.

column:store_purchases.extended_wholesal.description: The total cost of goods for this purchase line, equal to the wholesale cost per unit multiplied by the quantity. Used to calculate gross profit and margin at the line-item level.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.extended_wholesal.name: extended_wholesale_cost

column:store_purchases.household_profile.description: Links the store purchase to a household-level segment record in the household_profiles dimension, capturing attributes such as income range, buying potential, number of dependents, and vehicle count. column:store_purchases.list_price.description: The published or catalog price per unit for the merchandise item before any discounts or promotions are applied. Used to measure discount depth and compare actual selling prices against the standard price.

column:store_purchases.merchandise_ref.description: Links each store purchase line to a specific product record in the merchandise dimension. column:store_purchases.net_paid.description: The total amount the shopper actually paid for this purchase line after discounts and coupons, but before tax. This is the primary revenue measure for store sales analysis. Defined in the business context as the net amount paid by the shopper for the store purchase.

metadata.table_grain: One row per line item per store purchase transaction

column:store_purchases.net_paid.name: net_paid

column:store_purchases.net_paid_with_tax.description: The total amount the shopper paid for this purchase line including applicable sales tax. Used when tax-inclusive revenue figures are needed for financial reporting or regional tax analysis.

column:store_purchases.net_profit.description: The profit generated on this purchase line, calculated as the net amount paid minus the wholesale cost. metric:count_of_distinct_clients:33c988d.description: Metric used by pattern: Counts distinct customers grouped by household income band for a specified US state, joining clients to their current address and household profile to retrieve income range boundaries.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound, count(DISTINCT c.client_record) AS clients FROM clients c JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN household_profiles hp ON c.current_household_profile_ref = hp.household_profile_record JOIN income_ranges ir ON hp.income_range_ref = ir.income_range_record WHERE a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound ORDER BY clients DESC

metric:count_of_distinct_clients:33c988d.name: count of distinct clients
terms: how many customers in a given state fall into each household income band?

metric:count_of_distinct_customers:5061b.description: Metric used by pattern: Counts distinct online customers and sums net paid by marketing campaign and product category for a specified year and category, returning the top campaigns by customer count up to a specified row limit.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT mc.campaign_name, m.category, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN marketing_campaigns mc ON op.campaign_ref = mc.campaign_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY mc.campaign_name, m.category ORDER BY customers DESC LIMIT {{number}}

metric:count_of_distinct_customers:5061b.name: count of distinct customers
terms: count of distinct customers, sum of net paid
which marketing campaigns drove the most online customers and revenue for a product category in a given year?

metric:count_of_distinct_customers:6fe58.description: Metric used by pattern: Counts distinct customers across online, mail-order, and store purchase channels for a specified calendar year, combining all three channels via UNION ALL and grouping by channel.

metadata.sql_template: WITH channel_customers AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, op.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, mp.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, sp.client_ref AS client_ref FROM store_purchases sp JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} ) SELECT channel, count(DISTINCT client_ref) AS customers FROM channel_customers WHERE client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY channel ORDER BY customers DESC

metric:count_of_distinct_customers:6fe58.name: count of distinct customers
terms: how many distinct customers purchased through each sales channel in a given year?

metric:count_of_distinct_customers:e74f7.description: Metric used by pattern: Aggregates distinct customer counts and total net paid by online property name and site page type for a specified calendar year, returning the top results by revenue up to a specified row limit.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT op2.name AS online_property, sp.page_type, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN online_properties op2 ON op.online_property_ref = op2.online_property_record JOIN site_pages sp ON op.site_page_ref = sp.site_page_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY op2.name, sp.page_type ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}

metric:count_of_distinct_customers_curre.description: Metric used by pattern: Counts distinct online customers grouped by year, filtered to those whose current address is in a specified US state, and separately counts customers who shipped to or billed from another specified state during that year.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS current_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ship_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.shipping_client_ref END) AS shipping_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN bill_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.billing_client_ref END) AS billing_state_customers FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN addresses current_a ON c.current_address_ref = current_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses ship_a ON op.shipping_address_ref = ship_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses bill_a ON op.billing_address_ref = bill_a.address_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND current_a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year

terms: count of distinct customers (current address, shipping address, billing address)

metric:count_of_distinct_online_customer.description: Metric used by pattern: Aggregates distinct online customer counts and total net paid segmented by customer state, gender, and product category for a specified year, joining purchases to client demographics and address data.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS online_customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN client_profiles cp ON c.current_client_profile_ref = cp.client_profile_record JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND a.state = {{string}} AND cp.gender = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year

metric:count_of_distinct_orders:cc9500df.description: Metric used by pattern: Aggregates mail-order purchases for a specified calendar year by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center, returning the number of distinct orders and total net paid amount including delivery charges, ordered by net paid descending and limited to a specified number of rows.

metadata.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name AS support_center, count(DISTINCT mp.order_number) AS orders, sum(mp.net_paid_with_delivery) AS net_paid_with_delivery FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON mp.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record JOIN delivery_methods dm ON mp.delivery_method_ref = dm.delivery_method_record JOIN support_centers sc ON mp.support_center_ref = sc.support_center_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name ORDER BY net_paid_with_delivery DESC LIMIT {{number}}

metric:count_of_distinct_refunded_custom.description: Metric used by pattern: Aggregates refund amounts and distinct refunded customer counts across all three return channels (online, mail-order, store) for a specified product category and calendar year, grouped by channel and category.

metadata.sql_template: WITH refunds AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM online_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM mail_order_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, client_ref AS refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM store_refunds ) SELECT r.channel, m.category, count(DISTINCT r.refunded_client_ref) AS refunded_customers, sum(r.return_amount) AS refund_amount FROM refunds r JOIN merchandise m ON r.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON r.return_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY r.channel, m.category ORDER BY refund_amount DESC

metadata.sql_template: SELECT rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category, sum(sp.net_paid) AS net_paid, count(DISTINCT sp.client_ref) AS store_customers FROM store_purchases sp JOIN retail_locations rl ON sp.retail_location_ref = rl.retail_location_record JOIN merchandise m ON sp.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND rl.state = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}

metric:count_of_retained_online_customer.description: Metric used by pattern: Counts online customers who placed at least one purchase in each of two specified calendar years, measuring year-over-year retention for the online channel.

metadata.sql_template: WITH customer_years AS ( SELECT op.billing_client_ref, cd.year FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year IN ({{number}}, {{number}}) AND op.billing_client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY op.billing_client_ref, cd.year ) SELECT count(*) AS retained_online_customers FROM ( SELECT billing_client_ref FROM customer_years GROUP BY billing_client_ref HAVING count(DISTINCT year) = {{number}} ) retained

terms: how many online customers made purchases in both of two specified years?
