metadata.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year, sum(sl.quantity_on_hand) AS stock_on_hand FROM stock_levels sl JOIN merchandise m ON sl.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sl.calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON sl.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND cd.month_of_year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year ORDER BY stock_on_hand ASC LIMIT {{number}}

metadata.sql_template: WITH refunds AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM online_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM mail_order_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, client_ref AS refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM store_refunds ) SELECT r.channel, m.category, count(DISTINCT r.refunded_client_ref) AS refunded_customers, sum(r.return_amount) AS refund_amount FROM refunds r JOIN merchandise m ON r.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON r.return_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY r.channel, m.category ORDER BY refund_amount DESC

pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.description: Aggregates distinct online customer counts and total net paid segmented by customer state, gender, and product category for a specified year, joining purchases to client demographics and address data.

assumptions: Client's current address and current demographic profile are used for state and gender.
Gender values are expected to be 'M' or 'F' as string literals.
A single value is expected for each of state, gender, and category filters.
No ordering or limit is applied; all matching group combinations are returned.

filters: client address state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state, gender, product category, and year
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct online customers, sum of net paid

metadata.sql_template: SELECT a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS online_customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN client_profiles cp ON c.current_client_profile_ref = cp.client_profile_record JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND a.state = {{string}} AND cp.gender = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year

pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.name: How many online customers and how much net revenue came from a specific state, gender, and product category in a given year?
terms: how many online customers and how much net revenue came from a specific state, gender, and product category in a given year? aggregates distinct online customer counts and total net paid segmented by customer state, gender, and product category for a specified year, joining purchases to client demographics and address data. show distinct online buyers and total net paid by state, gender, and merchandise category for a specific year. what are the online sales and customer counts for a given state, customer gender, and product category in a specified year? break down online revenue and unique customers by state, gender segment, and category for a given year. ['state', 'gender', 'merchandise category', 'calendar year'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'client address state equals specified state', 'client gender equals specified gender', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per state, gender, product category, and year count of distinct online customers, sum of net paid single calendar year addresses calendar days client profiles clients merchandise online purchases addresses.address record addresses.state calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year client profiles.client profile record client profiles.gender clients.client record clients.current address ref clients.current client profile ref merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.merchandise ref online purchases.net paid online purchases.sale calendar day ref

pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.description: Counts distinct online customers grouped by year, filtered to those whose current address is in a specified US state, and separately counts customers who shipped to or billed from another specified state during that year.

assumptions: The primary population is filtered by the customer's current address state, not the transaction address state.
Shipping and billing state customer counts use conditional distinct counts against separate state filter values, which may differ from the current address state filter.
A customer can appear in multiple counts (current, shipping, billing) if their addresses match the respective state filters.
The query joins billing client to the clients table; shipping client is counted separately via the shipping address join.
Left joins are used for shipping and billing addresses to retain all purchases even when address data is missing.

filters: Calendar year equals a specified year
Customer current address state equals a specified state
intent_signature.measure: Count of distinct customers (current address, shipping address, billing address)

metadata.sql_template: SELECT cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS current_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ship_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.shipping_client_ref END) AS shipping_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN bill_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.billing_client_ref END) AS billing_state_customers FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN addresses current_a ON c.current_address_ref = current_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses ship_a ON op.shipping_address_ref = ship_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses bill_a ON op.billing_address_ref = bill_a.address_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND current_a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year

pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.name: How many unique customers from a given state made online purchases in a specific year, by shipping and billing state?
terms: how many unique customers from a given state made online purchases in a specific year, by shipping and billing state? counts distinct online customers grouped by year, filtered to those whose current address is in a specified us state, and separately counts customers who shipped to or billed from another specified state during that year. for a given year, how many distinct online buyers currently live in a specific state, and how many shipped to or billed from that state? what is the count of unique online customers by current address state, shipping state, and billing state for a particular year? show me the number of distinct online purchasers whose current address is in a given state, broken out by shipping and billing state matches, for a chosen year. ['calendar year', 'customer current address state', 'shipping address state', 'billing address state'] ['calendar year equals a specified year', 'customer current address state equals a specified state'] year count of distinct customers (current address, shipping address, billing address) single calendar year addresses calendar days clients online purchases addresses.address record addresses.state calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year clients.client record clients.current address ref online purchases.billing address ref online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.sale calendar day ref online purchases.shipping address ref online purchases.shipping client ref

pattern:history:addresses_clients_househ.description: Counts distinct customers grouped by household income band for a specified US state, joining clients to their current address and household profile to retrieve income range boundaries.

assumptions: Only the client's current address and current household profile are used.
Income bands are defined by lower_bound and upper_bound in the income_ranges table.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
No time filter is applied; reflects current demographic snapshot.

filters: client address state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state and income band
intent_signature.time_window: none (point-in-time based on current address and household profile)

metadata.sql_template: SELECT a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound, count(DISTINCT c.client_record) AS clients FROM clients c JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN household_profiles hp ON c.current_household_profile_ref = hp.household_profile_record JOIN income_ranges ir ON hp.income_range_ref = ir.income_range_record WHERE a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound ORDER BY clients DESC

pattern:history:addresses_clients_househ.name: How many customers in a given state fall into each household income band?
terms: how many customers in a given state fall into each household income band? counts distinct customers grouped by household income band for a specified us state, joining clients to their current address and household profile to retrieve income range boundaries. show the distribution of customers by income range for a specific us state. what is the customer count per income bracket for clients located in a given state? break down the number of distinct clients by household income band in a specified state. ['state', 'income range lower bound', 'income range upper bound'] ['client address state equals specified state'] one row per state and income band count of distinct clients none (point in time based on current address and household profile) addresses clients household profiles income ranges addresses.address record addresses.state clients.client record clients.current address ref clients.current household profile ref household profiles.household profile record household profiles.income range ref income ranges.income range record income ranges.lower bound income ranges.upper bound

pattern:history:calendar_days_delivery_m.description: Aggregates mail-order purchases for a specified calendar year by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center, returning the number of distinct orders and total net paid amount including delivery charges, ordered by net paid descending and limited to a specified number of rows.

assumptions: Order count is based on distinct order numbers, not line items, so multi-line orders are counted once.
Net paid with delivery includes delivery charges but excludes tax.
Results are ranked by net paid with delivery in descending order.
The number of rows returned is controlled by a configurable LIMIT value.
All joins (fulfillment center, delivery method, support center, calendar days) are inner joins, so purchases missing any of these references are excluded.

filters: Calendar year equals a specified year
intent_signature.grain: Fulfillment center × carrier × support center per year
intent_signature.measure: Count of distinct orders, sum of net paid with delivery

metadata.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name AS support_center, count(DISTINCT mp.order_number) AS orders, sum(mp.net_paid_with_delivery) AS net_paid_with_delivery FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON mp.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record JOIN delivery_methods dm ON mp.delivery_method_ref = dm.delivery_method_record JOIN support_centers sc ON mp.support_center_ref = sc.support_center_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name ORDER BY net_paid_with_delivery DESC LIMIT {{number}}

pattern:history:calendar_days_delivery_m.name: What are the top mail-order fulfillment centers and carriers by net paid revenue for a given year?
terms: what are the top mail order fulfillment centers and carriers by net paid revenue for a given year? aggregates mail order purchases for a specified calendar year by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center, returning the number of distinct orders and total net paid amount including delivery charges, ordered by net paid descending and limited to a specified number of rows. which warehouse, carrier, and support center combinations generated the most net paid revenue from mail order purchases in a specific year? show the top mail order order counts and net paid amounts by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center for a chosen year. rank mail order sales by warehouse name, carrier, and support center based on net paid with delivery for a given calendar year. ['fulfillment center warehouse name', 'delivery method carrier', 'support center name'] ['calendar year equals a specified year'] fulfillment center × carrier × support center per year count of distinct orders, sum of net paid with delivery single calendar year calendar days delivery methods fulfillment centers mail order purchases support centers calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year delivery methods.carrier delivery methods.delivery method record fulfillment centers.fulfillment center record fulfillment centers.warehouse name mail order purchases.delivery method ref mail order purchases.fulfillment center ref mail order purchases.net paid with delivery mail order purchases.order number mail order purchases.sale calendar day ref mail order purchases.support center ref support centers.name support centers.support center record

pattern:history:calendar_days_fulfillmen.description: Sums daily inventory quantity on hand by fulfillment center and product category for a specified year, month, and category, returning the lowest-stocked warehouses up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Stock levels are daily snapshots; summing across the month aggregates all daily records.
Results are ordered by stock on hand ascending (lowest first).
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
Filtering is applied on both year and month_of_year from the calendar_days dimension.

filters: month of year equals specified month
intent_signature.grain: one row per fulfillment center, product category, year, and month

metadata.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year, sum(sl.quantity_on_hand) AS stock_on_hand FROM stock_levels sl JOIN merchandise m ON sl.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sl.calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON sl.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND cd.month_of_year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year ORDER BY stock_on_hand ASC LIMIT {{number}}

pattern:history:calendar_days_fulfillmen.name: Which fulfillment centers have the lowest stock on hand for a product category in a given year and month?
terms: which fulfillment centers have the lowest stock on hand for a product category in a given year and month? sums daily inventory quantity on hand by fulfillment center and product category for a specified year, month, and category, returning the lowest stocked warehouses up to a specified row limit. show total inventory by warehouse for a specific product category, year, and month, sorted by lowest stock first. what are the bottom warehouses by quantity on hand for a given category and month? list fulfillment centers with the least inventory for a product category in a specific month and year. ['warehouse name', 'merchandise category', 'year', 'month of year'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'month of year equals specified month', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per fulfillment center, product category, year, and month sum of quantity on hand single calendar year and month calendar days fulfillment centers merchandise stock levels calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.month of year calendar days.year fulfillment centers.fulfillment center record fulfillment centers.warehouse name merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record stock levels.calendar day ref stock levels.fulfillment center ref stock levels.merchandise ref stock levels.quantity on hand

pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.description: Counts distinct customers across online, mail-order, and store purchase channels for a specified calendar year, combining all three channels via UNION ALL and grouping by channel.

assumptions: Channel labels are provided as string literals in the query.
Customers with NULL client references are excluded.
The same year filter is applied independently to each channel.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
metadata.sql_template: WITH channel_customers AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, op.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, mp.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, sp.client_ref AS client_ref FROM store_purchases sp JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} ) SELECT channel, count(DISTINCT client_ref) AS customers FROM channel_customers WHERE client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY channel ORDER BY customers DESC

pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.name: How many distinct customers purchased through each sales channel in a given year?
terms: how many distinct customers purchased through each sales channel in a given year? counts distinct customers across online, mail order, and store purchase channels for a specified calendar year, combining all three channels via union all and grouping by channel. what is the unique customer count per channel (online, mail order, store) for a specific year? show me the number of distinct buyers by sales channel for a given calendar year. which channel had the most customers in a particular year? ['sales channel (online, mail order, store)'] ['calendar year equals specified year'] one row per sales channel count of distinct customers single calendar year calendar days mail order purchases online purchases store purchases calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year mail order purchases.billing client ref mail order purchases.sale calendar day ref online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.sale calendar day ref store purchases.client ref store purchases.sale calendar day ref

pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.description: Aggregates refund amounts and distinct refunded customer counts across all three return channels (online, mail-order, store) for a specified product category and calendar year, grouped by channel and category.

assumptions: Channel labels are provided as string literals.
All three refund tables (online_refunds, mail_order_refunds, store_refunds) are combined via UNION ALL.
Results are ordered by refund amount descending.
The store_refunds table uses 'client_ref' while the other tables use 'refunded_client_ref'.

intent_signature.grain: one row per channel and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of refund amount, count of distinct refunded customers

metadata.sql_template: WITH refunds AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM online_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM mail_order_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, client_ref AS refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM store_refunds ) SELECT r.channel, m.category, count(DISTINCT r.refunded_client_ref) AS refunded_customers, sum(r.return_amount) AS refund_amount FROM refunds r JOIN merchandise m ON r.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON r.return_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY r.channel, m.category ORDER BY refund_amount DESC

pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.name: What are the total refund amounts and distinct refunded customers by channel and product category for a given year?
terms: what are the total refund amounts and distinct refunded customers by channel and product category for a given year? aggregates refund amounts and distinct refunded customer counts across all three return channels (online, mail order, store) for a specified product category and calendar year, grouped by channel and category. show refund totals and unique refunded customer counts per sales channel and merchandise category for a specific year. how much was refunded and how many customers received refunds by channel and category in a given year? break down refund amounts and refunded customer counts across online, mail order, and store channels for a product category and year. ['sales channel (online, mail order, store)', 'merchandise category'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per channel and product category sum of refund amount, count of distinct refunded customers single calendar year calendar days mail order refunds merchandise online refunds store refunds calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record

pattern:history:calendar_days_marketing_.description: Counts distinct online customers and sums net paid by marketing campaign and product category for a specified year and category, returning the top campaigns by customer count up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Only online purchases with a campaign reference are included.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
A single merchandise category filter is applied.

intent_signature.grain: one row per marketing campaign and product category
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct customers, sum of net paid

metadata.sql_template: SELECT mc.campaign_name, m.category, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN marketing_campaigns mc ON op.campaign_ref = mc.campaign_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY mc.campaign_name, m.category ORDER BY customers DESC LIMIT {{number}}

pattern:history:calendar_days_marketing_.name: Which marketing campaigns drove the most online customers and revenue for a product category in a given year?
terms: which marketing campaigns drove the most online customers and revenue for a product category in a given year? counts distinct online customers and sums net paid by marketing campaign and product category for a specified year and category, returning the top campaigns by customer count up to a specified row limit. show distinct online buyers and net paid by campaign and merchandise category for a specific year. what are the top campaigns by customer count for a given product category and year? rank marketing campaigns by unique online customers for a specified category and calendar year. ['campaign name', 'merchandise category'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per marketing campaign and product category count of distinct customers, sum of net paid single calendar year calendar days marketing campaigns merchandise online purchases calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year marketing campaigns.campaign name marketing campaigns.campaign record merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.campaign ref online purchases.merchandise ref online purchases.net paid online purchases.sale calendar day ref

pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.description: Calculates total net paid, total net profit, and profit margin ratio for online purchases filtered to a specific product category and calendar year.

assumptions: Profit margin is computed as sum(net_profit) / nullif(sum(net_paid), 0) to avoid division by zero.
Only the online channel is included; no cross-channel comparison.
A single category filter is applied; results return one row per year/category combination.

intent_signature.grain: one row per year and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of net paid, sum of net profit, profit margin (net profit / net paid)

metadata.sql_template: SELECT cd.year, m.category, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid, sum(op.net_profit) AS net_profit, sum(op.net_profit) / nullif(sum(op.net_paid), {{number}}) AS profit_margin FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year, m.category

pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.name: What is the net revenue and profit margin for online sales by product category in a given year?
terms: what is the net revenue and profit margin for online sales by product category in a given year? calculates total net paid, total net profit, and profit margin ratio for online purchases filtered to a specific product category and calendar year. show total net paid, net profit, and profit margin for online purchases by category for a specific year. what are the online channel revenue and profitability metrics per product category in a given year? how profitable were online sales for each merchandise category in a specified year? ['calendar year', 'merchandise category'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per year and product category sum of net paid, sum of net profit, profit margin (net profit / net paid) single calendar year calendar days merchandise online purchases calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record online purchases.merchandise ref online purchases.net paid online purchases.net profit online purchases.sale calendar day ref

pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.description: Sums net paid and counts distinct customers by retail store and product category for a specified state, category, and calendar year, returning the top stores by revenue up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Results are ordered by net paid descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
State filter applies to the retail location's state, not the customer's address.
A single merchandise category filter is applied.

filters: retail location state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state, store name, and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of net paid, count of distinct store customers

metadata.sql_template: SELECT rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category, sum(sp.net_paid) AS net_paid, count(DISTINCT sp.client_ref) AS store_customers FROM store_purchases sp JOIN retail_locations rl ON sp.retail_location_ref = rl.retail_location_record JOIN merchandise m ON sp.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND rl.state = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}

pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.name: Which stores in a given state had the highest net sales for a product category in a specific year?
terms: which stores in a given state had the highest net sales for a product category in a specific year? sums net paid and counts distinct customers by retail store and product category for a specified state, category, and calendar year, returning the top stores by revenue up to a specified row limit. show net paid and distinct customer counts by store and merchandise category for a given state and year. what are the top retail locations by revenue for a specific product category, state, and year? rank stores in a given state by net sales for a particular merchandise category and calendar year. ['state', 'store name', 'merchandise category'] ['calendar year equals specified year', 'retail location state equals specified state', 'merchandise category equals specified category'] one row per state, store name, and product category sum of net paid, count of distinct store customers single calendar year calendar days merchandise retail locations store purchases calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year merchandise.category merchandise.merchandise record retail locations.retail location record retail locations.state retail locations.store name store purchases.client ref store purchases.merchandise ref store purchases.net paid store purchases.retail location ref store purchases.sale calendar day ref

pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pro.description: Aggregates distinct customer counts and total net paid by online property name and site page type for a specified calendar year, returning the top results by revenue up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Results are ordered by net paid descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
Page type comes from the site_pages table joined via site_page_ref.
Online property name comes from the online_properties table.

intent_signature.grain: one row per online property and page type
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct customers, sum of net paid

metadata.sql_template: SELECT op2.name AS online_property, sp.page_type, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN online_properties op2 ON op.online_property_ref = op2.online_property_record JOIN site_pages sp ON op.site_page_ref = sp.site_page_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY op2.name, sp.page_type ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}

pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pro.name: Which online properties and page types generated the most revenue and customers in a given year?
terms: which online properties and page types generated the most revenue and customers in a given year? aggregates distinct customer counts and total net paid by online property name and site page type for a specified calendar year, returning the top results by revenue up to a specified row limit. show net paid and distinct customer counts by website and page type for a specific year. what are the top online properties by revenue and unique buyers, broken down by page type, for a given year? rank online properties and site page types by net revenue for a specified calendar year. ['online property name', 'site page type'] ['calendar year equals specified year'] one row per online property and page type count of distinct customers, sum of net paid single calendar year calendar days online properties online purchases site pages calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year online properties.name online properties.online property record online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.net paid online purchases.online property ref online purchases.sale calendar day ref online purchases.site page ref site pages.site page record

pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pur.description: Counts online customers who placed at least one purchase in each of two specified calendar years, measuring year-over-year retention for the online channel.

assumptions: Retention is defined as having purchases in both specified years.
The HAVING clause checks that the customer appears in exactly the number of distinct years provided (2).
NULL billing client references are excluded.
No channel breakdown is returned; result is a single scalar count.

filters: calendar year is one of two specified years
billing client reference is not null

metadata.sql_template: WITH customer_years AS ( SELECT op.billing_client_ref, cd.year FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year IN ({{number}}, {{number}}) AND op.billing_client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY op.billing_client_ref, cd.year ) SELECT count(*) AS retained_online_customers FROM ( SELECT billing_client_ref FROM customer_years GROUP BY billing_client_ref HAVING count(DISTINCT year) = {{number}} ) retained

pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pur.name: How many online customers made purchases in both of two specified years?
terms: how many online customers made purchases in both of two specified years? counts online customers who placed at least one purchase in each of two specified calendar years, measuring year over year retention for the online channel. what is the count of retained online buyers who purchased in two consecutive years? how many online customers were active in both year a and year b? show the number of online shoppers who returned across two given years. ['sale calendar day ref'] ['calendar year is one of two specified years', 'billing client reference is not null'] single aggregate count count of retained online customers two specified calendar years calendar days online purchases calendar days.calendar day record calendar days.year online purchases.billing client ref online purchases.sale calendar day ref

table:addresses.description: This table stores the physical and mailing addresses associated with clients. Each record represents a unique address and includes full street details, city, county, state, ZIP code, country, timezone offset, and the type of dwelling (apartment, condo, or single family). It is used to support geographic segmentation and regional analysis of customers across purchases, refunds, and marketing activity. All addresses in this dataset are located in the United States. The table links to client records, billing addresses, and shipping addresses across the store, online, and mail-order purchase channels.

metadata.grain: One row per unique address

table:addresses.name: addresses
terms: this table stores the physical and mailing addresses associated with clients. each record represents a unique address and includes full street details, city, county, state, zip code, country, timezone offset, and the type of dwelling (apartment, condo, or single family). it is used to support geographic segmentation and regional analysis of customers across purchases, refunds, and marketing activity. all addresses in this dataset are located in the united states. the table links to client records, billing addresses, and shipping addresses across the store, online, and mail order purchase channels.

table:calendar_days.description: This table provides a comprehensive date dimension covering every calendar day from 1900 through 2100. Each row represents a single date and includes attributes for standard calendar reporting (year, quarter, month, week, day of week, day of month) as well as fiscal calendar equivalents (fiscal year, fiscal quarter sequence, fiscal week sequence). It also includes flags and indicators for holidays, weekends, days following a holiday, first and last days of the month, and whether a date falls within the current day, week, month, quarter, or year. Cross-period comparison fields such as same day last year and same day last quarter support year-over-year and quarter-over-quarter trend analysis. This table is referenced by purchase, refund, stock level, and campaign tables to enable time-based reporting.

metadata.grain: One row per calendar day

table:calendar_days.name: calendar_days
terms: this table provides a comprehensive date dimension covering every calendar day from 1900 through 2100. each row represents a single date and includes attributes for standard calendar reporting (year, quarter, month, week, day of week, day of month) as well as fiscal calendar equivalents (fiscal year, fiscal quarter sequence, fiscal week sequence). it also includes flags and indicators for holidays, weekends, days following a holiday, first and last days of the month, and whether a date falls within the current day, week, month, quarter, or year. cross period comparison fields such as same day last year and same day last quarter support year over year and quarter over quarter trend analysis. this table is referenced by purchase, refund, stock level, and campaign tables to enable time based reporting.

table:client_profiles.description: This table contains demographic profile records used to classify individual shoppers into segments for analysis. Each record captures a combination of demographic attributes: gender (male or female), marital status (single, married, divorced, widowed, or unknown), education level (ranging from primary through advanced degree), an estimated annual purchase amount, credit rating (Good, Low Risk, High Risk, or Unknown), and counts of total dependents, employed dependents, and dependents in college. With nearly 1.9 million rows, this table represents a large set of demographic combinations. Client records link to their current active profile via a reference key. These segments are used to analyze purchasing behavior, revenue, and campaign response across different customer groups.

metadata.grain: One row per unique demographic segment combination

table:client_profiles.name: client_profiles
terms: this table contains demographic profile records used to classify individual shoppers into segments for analysis. each record captures a combination of demographic attributes: gender (male or female), marital status (single, married, divorced, widowed, or unknown), education level (ranging from primary through advanced degree), an estimated annual purchase amount, credit rating (good, low risk, high risk, or unknown), and counts of total dependents, employed dependents, and dependents in college. with nearly 1.9 million rows, this table represents a large set of demographic combinations. client records link to their current active profile via a reference key. these segments are used to analyze purchasing behavior, revenue, and campaign response across different customer groups.

table:clients.description: The clients table is the central record for every shopper or customer account in the business. Each row represents one person and links to their current mailing address, demographic profile, and household profile. The table also captures key tenure milestones such as the date of the customer's first purchase and first shipment, along with personal details like name, salutation, date of birth, birth country, email address, and a preferred-customer flag. It serves as the starting point for customer segmentation, loyalty analysis, lifetime value reporting, and cross-channel purchase history.

metadata.grain: One row per customer account

table:clients.name: clients
terms: the clients table is the central record for every shopper or customer account in the business. each row represents one person and links to their current mailing address, demographic profile, and household profile. the table also captures key tenure milestones such as the date of the customer's first purchase and first shipment, along with personal details like name, salutation, date of birth, birth country, email address, and a preferred customer flag. it serves as the starting point for customer segmentation, loyalty analysis, lifetime value reporting, and cross channel purchase history.

table:clock_times.description: The clock_times table is a time-of-day dimension containing one row for every second in a 24-hour day (86,400 rows total). It supports analysis of when purchases, returns, or other events occur throughout the day. Each record provides the exact hour, minute, and second, along with AM/PM indicator, work shift (first, second, third), sub-shift period (morning, afternoon, evening, night), and meal period (breakfast, lunch, dinner). This table is used to answer questions such as which shift drives the most sales or whether purchases spike during lunch hours.

metadata.grain: One row per second of the day

table:clock_times.name: clock_times
terms: the clock times table is a time of day dimension containing one row for every second in a 24 hour day (86,400 rows total). it supports analysis of when purchases, returns, or other events occur throughout the day. each record provides the exact hour, minute, and second, along with am/pm indicator, work shift (first, second, third), sub shift period (morning, afternoon, evening, night), and meal period (breakfast, lunch, dinner). this table is used to answer questions such as which shift drives the most sales or whether purchases spike during lunch hours.

table:delivery_methods.description: The delivery_methods table is a small reference table describing the 20 shipping and delivery options available to customers. Each record identifies the service type (such as EXPRESS, NEXT DAY, OVERNIGHT, REGULAR, or TWO DAY), the transport mode or code (AIR, BIKE, SEA, SURFACE), the carrier name (such as FedEx, UPS, DHL, or USPS), and the associated carrier contract identifier. This table is used to analyze order fulfillment by shipping speed, carrier performance, and delivery channel across store, online, and mail-order purchases.

metadata.grain: One row per delivery method

table:delivery_methods.name: delivery_methods
terms: the delivery methods table is a small reference table describing the 20 shipping and delivery options available to customers. each record identifies the service type (such as express, next day, overnight, regular, or two day), the transport mode or code (air, bike, sea, surface), the carrier name (such as fedex, ups, dhl, or usps), and the associated carrier contract identifier. this table is used to analyze order fulfillment by shipping speed, carrier performance, and delivery channel across store, online, and mail order purchases.

table:fulfillment_centers.description: The fulfillment_centers table describes the five warehouse or distribution center locations used by the business to store inventory and ship orders. Each record includes the warehouse name, physical size in square feet, and a full mailing address (street, city, county, state, ZIP, country, and timezone offset). This table is referenced by purchase and stock-level records to identify which warehouse fulfilled an order or holds a given product. All five centers in the current data appear to be located in Fairview, TN, United States. Some address and size fields have a small number of null values.

metadata.grain: One row per fulfillment center

table:household_profiles.description: The household_profiles table contains segment records that classify households along key economic and lifestyle dimensions. Each record describes a unique combination of income range, estimated buying potential, number of dependents, and vehicle count. These segments are linked to clients through the clients table and are used to analyze purchasing behavior, target marketing campaigns, and understand the economic profile of shoppers. Buying potential is expressed as a dollar range (e.g., '1001-5000', '>10000'), making it useful for segmenting high-value versus low-value households. The table contains 7,200 distinct segment combinations and serves as a reference dimension for retail analytics across all purchase and refund channels.

table:income_ranges.description: The income_ranges table is a small reference dimension containing 20 income band records. Each record defines a contiguous income bracket with a lower bound and an upper bound expressed in dollars (e.g., $0–$10,000 up to $190,001–$200,000). These bands are used to classify households into economic tiers via the household_profiles table. Analysts and business users can use this table to filter or group customers by income level, compare purchasing behavior across income segments, or evaluate campaign effectiveness by economic tier. The bands are evenly spaced at $10,000 intervals, covering a range from $0 to $200,000.

table:mail_order_purchases.description: The mail_order_purchases table records every item sold through the mail-order (catalog or mailer) channel. With over 1.4 million rows, it is the primary fact table for mail-order commerce analysis. Each row represents a line item within an order and captures the full financial picture: wholesale cost, list price, actual sales price, extended amounts for quantity, discounts, delivery costs, tax, and multiple net-paid totals. Profitability is available via net_profit. The table links to calendar days for sale date and shipping date, clock times for time-of-day analysis, clients (both billing and shipping), demographic profiles (client and household), addresses (billing and shipping), the support center that handled the order, the mailer page that prompted the purchase, the delivery method used, the fulfillment center that shipped the order, the merchandise item sold, and the marketing campaign associated with the sale. This makes it suitable for analyzing mail-order revenue, order volume, discount effectiveness, coupon usage, delivery costs, campaign ROI, customer segments, and fulfillment performance.

table:mail_order_refunds.description: This table records every item return or refund processed through the mail-order (catalog) channel. Each row represents a single return event and includes the date and time of the return, the product returned, the order it came from, and the quantity sent back. Two client roles are tracked: the client who was originally billed or refunded (refunded client) and the client who physically sent the item back (returning client). Both roles are linked to their demographic profiles and household profiles at the time of the return, enabling segmentation analysis. Financial columns cover the gross return amount, applicable taxes, processing fees, shipping costs for the return, and the breakdown of how the refund was issued—cash refund, charge reversal, or store credit. The net loss column summarizes the total financial impact of the return to the business. Support center, mailer page, delivery method, and fulfillment center references allow analysis of which operational units handled the return. Return reason links to a reference table describing why the item was sent back.

table:mailer_pages.description: This table describes individual pages within printed catalogs or mailers distributed to customers. Each record represents one page within a specific mailer issue and includes the mailer number (which catalog edition) and the page number within that mailer. Date references indicate when the mailer was active—its start and end calendar days—allowing analysis of which catalog editions drove purchases or returns during a given period. The department field categorizes the page by merchandise department, though the current data shows a placeholder value, suggesting this field may not yet be fully populated. The description field provides a free-text summary of the page content. The type field classifies the mailer cadence as monthly, quarterly, or bi-annual, which is useful for understanding promotion frequency and seasonality. This table is used as a dimension in mail-order purchase and refund analysis to connect transactions back to the specific catalog page that influenced the sale or return.

table:marketing_campaigns.description: The marketing_campaigns table contains one record per marketing campaign or promotional offer run by the business. Each campaign has a name, a start and end date, a budget cost, and flags indicating which channels were used to reach customers — such as direct mail, email, mailer, TV, radio, press, event, or profile-based targeting. Campaigns can be linked to a specific merchandise item being promoted. The table supports analysis of campaign reach, channel mix, promotional timing, and the relationship between marketing activity and sales or revenue outcomes. The purpose field is currently recorded as 'Unknown' for most campaigns, and the discount_active flag indicates whether a discount was active during the campaign. With 300 campaigns in the dataset, this table is the primary reference for marketing attribution across store, online, and mail-order purchase channels.

table:merchandise.description: The merchandise table is the central product catalog for the business, containing up to 18,000 product records. Each record describes a sellable item with attributes including product name, description, brand, product class, category, manufacturer, size, color, formulation, units of measure, and container type. Pricing information includes the current retail price and wholesale cost. The table supports slowly changing history through record_effective_start and record_effective_end dates, allowing analysis of how product attributes or pricing changed over time. Categories span Books, Children, Electronics, Home, Jewelry, Men, Music, Shoes, Sports, and Women. Classes include detailed groupings such as kids, shirts, fragrances, swimwear, dresses, and many others. This table is joined to purchase, refund, stock level, and campaign tables to analyze sales performance, profitability, inventory, and promotional effectiveness by product, brand, category, or manufacturer.

table:online_properties.description: The online_properties table contains one record per website or digital commerce property operated by the business. It supports online-channel analysis by capturing the site name, opening and closure dates, responsible manager, market grouping, and company affiliation. Address fields (street, city, state, ZIP, country) describe the registered or operational location of each digital property. The table uses slowly changing dimension logic, with record_effective_start and record_effective_end dates tracking historical versions of each property's attributes. A null record_effective_end indicates the currently active version. Tax percentage is stored at the property level to support revenue and tax calculations for online purchases. With 30 rows covering 5 distinct site names, this is a small reference dimension used to group and filter online purchase and refund activity by website, market region, or company.

table:online_purchases.description: The online_purchases table records every line-item sale completed through the business's website or digital commerce properties. With nearly 720,000 rows, it captures the full financial picture of each online order: the product sold, the customer who placed and received the order, the website and page where the purchase originated, the marketing campaign associated with the sale, the delivery method chosen, and the fulfillment center that shipped the goods. Financial columns cover unit-level pricing (wholesale cost, list price, sales price) as well as extended amounts scaled by quantity, including discounts, taxes, delivery costs, coupon savings, and multiple net-paid totals. A net profit column allows margin analysis at the transaction level. Dimension references connect each purchase to calendar dates for both sale and shipping timing, client demographic and household profiles for both the billing and shipping parties, and their respective addresses. This table is the primary source for online channel revenue, discount, coupon, delivery cost, and profitability analysis.

table:online_refunds.description: The online_refunds table records every item return or refund processed through the website or digital commerce channel. Each row represents a single online return event and captures when the return occurred (date and time), which product was returned, and the two client roles involved: the client who was originally billed (refunded client) and the client who physically submitted the return (returning client). Both client roles are linked to their demographic profiles, household profiles, and addresses at the time of the return, enabling segmentation analysis by geography, income range, and household characteristics. The table also links to the website page associated with the return, the reason the item was returned, and the original order number. Financial columns cover the gross return amount, applicable taxes, total return amount with tax, any processing fees, shipping costs for the return, and the three forms of reimbursement issued: cash refund, reversed charge (credit card reversal), and account credit. The net_loss column summarizes the overall financial loss to the business from the return. This table is the primary source for online return rate analysis, refund method breakdowns, return reason reporting, and customer return behavior studies.

table:retail_locations.description: This table contains one record per physical retail store (or store version over time), capturing everything needed to analyze the brick-and-mortar sales channel. Each row describes a store's name, size, staffing, operating hours, manager, and full mailing address including city, state, ZIP, county, and country. Market grouping attributes such as market code, market description, and market manager support regional and territory analysis. Division and company fields are present but currently show a single unknown value, suggesting they may not yet be populated. A slowly-changing-dimension pattern is evident: record_effective_start and record_effective_end dates track when each version of a store's attributes was active, and closure_calendar_day_ref records when a store closed. The tax_percentage field supports sales tax calculations by store location. With only 12 rows, this table represents a small chain of physical stores.

table:return_reasons.description: This small reference table contains 35 distinct return or refund reason codes and their plain-language descriptions. It is used across store, online, and mail-order refund transactions to explain why a shopper returned a product. Example reasons include 'Did not fit', 'Wrong size', 'Package was damaged', 'Found a better price in a store', 'Stopped working', 'Gift exchange', and 'Unauthorized purchase'. Each reason has a unique business-facing code and a human-readable description. This table is joined to refund fact tables to enable analysis of return drivers, product quality issues, pricing competitiveness, and fulfillment problems.

table:site_pages.description: This table holds one record per website page (or page version over time), capturing attributes used to analyze the online shopping experience. Each page is described by its type (such as general, welcome, ad, order, feedback, dynamic, or protected), URL, creation date, most recent access date, and content metrics including character count, number of links, number of images, and maximum ad slots. An auto-generated flag distinguishes system-created pages from manually authored ones. A client reference links some pages to specific shoppers, though roughly 65% of pages have no client association, suggesting most pages are public or shared. The slowly-changing-dimension pattern (record_effective_start and record_effective_end) tracks page attribute changes over time. This table supports analysis of website content strategy, page engagement timing, and ad inventory capacity.

table:stock_levels.description: This large fact-style table records the quantity of each merchandise item on hand at each fulfillment center for each calendar day. With nearly 12 million rows, it provides a comprehensive view of inventory availability across the warehouse network. Each row links a specific product (via merchandise_ref), a warehouse location (via fulfillment_center_ref), and a calendar date (via calendar_day_ref) to a quantity_on_hand figure ranging from 0 to 1,000. Approximately 5% of quantity_on_hand values are null, which may indicate missing snapshots or items not tracked on certain days. The table covers all 5 fulfillment centers and all 18,000 merchandise items, making it the primary source for inventory analysis, stockout detection, replenishment planning, and supply-demand comparisons against purchase volumes.

table:support_centers.description: The support_centers table contains one record per customer service or support center location used by the business to assist shoppers, particularly in mail-order and assisted commerce operations. Each record describes a support center's physical address, size in square feet, number of employees, operating hours, and the manager responsible.
